evals.notes: Both versions produced excellent blueprint content. Neither used FigJam MCP — both fell back to Mermaid. Skill's primary differentiator (FigJam) not triggered.
Critical: Step 0 (mandatory confirmation) was skipped by the with-skill agent. It built immediately. Guardrails content is excellent (1/5 passed). Baseline blocked by permissions.
Both produced functional .docx reports. With-skill version has significantly better formatting (dark blue headers, shaded transcript excerpts, prominent summary score, Inspra branding).

analyst_observations: FigJam MCP assertion (blueprint) failed for both with-skill and without-skill — this suggests the skill instructions aren't strong enough to override the model's default Mermaid preference, OR the MCP tool wasn't available in the test environment.
Step 0 confirmation (prototype) is the biggest gap — the skill explicitly says MANDATORY but the agent skipped it entirely. This needs much stronger instruction framing.
Reports skill is the strongest performer — 5/5 assertions passed with excellent formatting. The skill instructions are working well here.
The prototype with-skill run produced .md instead of .docx despite the skill specifying .docx output — another instruction adherence issue.
Baseline comparison is partially inconclusive for prototype (permissions blocked) and limited for reports (both passed all assertions).
